F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: Before entering a home or a Buddhist temple, the visitor removes shoes or sandals, and in raised houses leaves shoes at the bottom of the stairs before going up.

GPT-5.2.name: Anoulack Phongsavath
GPT-5.2.story:
Anoulack took a short trip to Luang Prabang with friends and visited a small Buddhist temple in the late morning. At the entrance, he saw several pairs of sandals lined neatly near the steps. He stopped on the stone path and slipped his sandals off, placing them beside the others with the straps facing outward.

Inside the temple courtyard, he walked in socks across the cool tiles, keeping his hands at his sides. Afterward, his friend invited him to meet an aunt in a nearby neighborhood of raised wooden houses.

They arrived at a home with a narrow staircase leading up to the main floor. Anoulack climbed halfway, noticed shoes clustered at the bottom, and stepped back down one stair. He left his sandals on the ground beside the other pairs before going up again barefoot.

At the top, the host opened the door and smiled. “Come in,” she said.

Anoulack nodded and stepped onto the wooden floor without footwear. He walked carefully past woven mats and a low table where fruit and tea were set out. When he needed to step out onto the small balcony, he remained barefoot, moving slowly over the wooden boards.

A child ran down the stairs, dodging the pile of shoes. Anoulack shifted his own sandals slightly to the side at the bottom so the path stayed clear.

When it was time to leave, he walked back down, sat on the lowest step, and put his sandals on there before stepping onto the yard.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the visitor needs footwear for medical support, the visitor asks for guidance and uses clean covers or follows the host’s preferred alternative.

GPT-5.2.name: Mayuree Sisouphanthong
GPT-5.2.story:
Mayuree visited Wat Si Saket in Vientiane with her aunt and wore supportive shoes for a foot condition. At the entrance, she saw the line of sandals and paused. She turned to the temple attendant and asked softly, “I need my shoes for support. What should I do?”

The attendant looked at her shoes and nodded toward a small basket. “Covers,” he said in English, lifting two thin protective covers.

Mayuree thanked him and sat on a low step. She slipped the clean covers over her shoes, stretching the elastic around the soles. She stood carefully, tested her balance, and then entered the temple area.

Later, she was invited to a relative’s raised home in a nearby village. At the bottom of the stairs, relatives were leaving sandals in a neat cluster. Mayuree stopped beside the stair post and told the host, “My shoes help me walk. Is it okay if I keep them with covers, or should I stay here?”

The host glanced at the covers and said, “Okay, with covers.”

Mayuree adjusted the covers again to ensure they were clean and secure, then went up slowly, keeping her steps close to the edge of the stairs. Inside, she stayed on the wooden floor path near the doorway as directed, avoiding the sleeping area where pillows were stacked.

When she prepared to leave, she removed the covers at the bottom step, folded them, and placed them back into her bag before putting her shoes fully on for the walk home.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The visitor removes shoes correctly, but then complains loudly about the practice, making the host feel criticized.

GPT-5.2.name: Tomas Vongsouthi
GPT-5.2.story:
Tomas visited a friend’s family home in Luang Prabang for dinner. The house was raised, with a wooden staircase leading up. At the bottom of the stairs, several pairs of sandals were lined neatly.

Tomas sighed dramatically, bent down, and slipped off his shoes. “Everywhere I go, always shoes off,” he said loudly, holding one shoe up as if showing it to the group. He placed them beside the other pairs and climbed the stairs barefoot.

At the top, the friend’s mother opened the door and smiled. Tomas stepped inside without shoes, then looked around and said, “Your floor must be so sensitive. In my place, nobody worries like this.”

The host kept her smile and gestured toward the mats. “Sit here,” she said.

Tomas sat, then called toward the kitchen, “It’s kind of annoying, you know. My feet get dusty.” He wiggled his toes and laughed. A cousin poured tea and set a cup near him.

When another guest arrived and started to remove sandals, Tomas pointed and said, “See? Now you have to do the shoe thing too,” in a joking tone.

The host’s mother adjusted the tray and said nothing. Tomas kept talking about how he preferred “normal houses,” then leaned forward to grab a snack.

At the end, he walked down quickly and said, “Finally,” as he shoved his feet back into his shoes at the bottom step.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: Before entering a home or a Buddhist temple, the visitor keeps footwear on and walks inside, ignoring the household’s entry practice.

GPT-5.2.name: Jordan Phengsavanh
GPT-5.2.story:
Jordan toured a temple in Vientiane with a local guide on a hot afternoon. Near the entrance, there was a row of sandals and shoes on the ground. The guide stepped out of his sandals and left them by the steps.

Jordan glanced at the footwear, but kept his sneakers on and walked up the steps anyway. The soles made a soft squeak on the tile as he moved inside. A woman near the door looked down at his shoes and then turned her face away.

He wandered across the tiled floor toward a corner, pausing to take a photo. The guide said quietly, “Shoes off,” and gestured toward the entrance.

Jordan waved him off. “I’ll be quick,” he said, still walking.

Later that day, the guide brought Jordan to a raised wooden house to meet an artisan. At the bottom of the stairs, many sandals were arranged neatly. Jordan climbed the stairs in his sneakers, stepping over the shoe line, and continued up.

At the top, the artisan opened the door and hesitated when Jordan stepped onto the wooden floor with shoes on. Jordan smiled and said, “Nice place,” then walked straight toward a low mat without taking his shoes off.

The artisan’s wife picked up a broom and began sweeping near the doorway while watching Jordan’s footprints.

Jordan sat on a cushion and bounced his sneakered foot lightly, tapping the wooden floor as he looked around.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: In a traditional home, the visitor sits on cushions or low seats and avoids stretching legs toward others while seated on the floor.

GPT-5.2.name: Chanthaly Phommasone
GPT-5.2.story:
Chanthaly visited her uncle’s traditional wooden home outside Pakse for a family visit. Inside, woven mats covered the floor, and several cushions were arranged around a low tray with fruit and tea. Two older relatives sat facing the center, knees folded and feet tucked to the side.

Chanthaly removed her sandals at the bottom step, climbed up, and entered quietly. She chose an open cushion near the edge and lowered herself onto it, folding her legs to the side rather than stretching them forward. When she shifted her weight, she kept her toes angled toward the wall.

Her uncle’s friend sat across from her, and Chanthaly kept her knees turned slightly inward so the soles of her feet stayed tucked behind her skirt. She reached forward with both hands to accept a cup of tea, then rested it on the tray.

A younger cousin scooted in beside her, and Chanthaly made space by sliding her cushion back a little, still keeping her legs folded. When someone asked her to move closer for a group photo, she shuffled on her cushion rather than extending her legs toward anyone.

The conversation moved to travel plans, and Chanthaly nodded, sipping tea carefully. As the tray was rearranged, she lifted her cup with both hands, waited, and set it down again.

When it was time for dinner, she stood smoothly, stepped off the mat without brushing others, and carried an empty cup toward the kitchen area.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the visitor cannot sit comfortably on the floor, the visitor asks for a low chair, sits modestly, and keeps legs angled away from others.

GPT-5.2.name: Phoumi Xayasone
GPT-5.2.story:
Phoumi visited a friend’s home in Thakhek for a small tea gathering. The group was seated on mats around a low tray, and Phoumi’s back tightened when he tried to fold into a floor posture. He paused at the doorway and spoke to the host.

“Could I use a low chair? My back can’t sit on the floor long,” Phoumi said quietly.

The host nodded and brought a small low stool from the corner. “Here,” she said, placing it near the edge of the mat.

Phoumi sat on the stool with his feet flat on the mat, knees together, and angled his legs toward the wall rather than toward the people across from him. He adjusted the stool slightly so his toes pointed away from the older relatives.

When tea was served, Phoumi accepted the cup with both hands and kept it close to his lap. He didn’t stretch his legs out, and when he needed to shift, he scooted the stool a few centimeters rather than extending his feet.

A child crawled near the tray, and Phoumi lifted his heels briefly and set them down again, keeping his feet tucked under the stool. “Sorry,” he said softly as he adjusted.

As fruit was passed around, Phoumi leaned forward from the hips, using his hands to support the motion, then sat back upright with his legs still angled away.

When the gathering ended, he stood slowly, thanked the host, and carried the low stool back to its place by the wall.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The visitor adopts the local sitting posture, but repeatedly mocks it as uncomfortable, undermining the courtesy.

GPT-5.2.name: Nina Sisanone
GPT-5.2.story:
Nina visited a friend’s family home in Luang Prabang and was invited to sit on mats with the women in the front room. Cushions were laid out around a low tray. Nina removed her sandals and sat down, folding her legs to the side to match the others.

She adjusted her skirt and said, “Okay, I’m doing it,” then immediately winced and rubbed her calf. “How do you all sit like this? My legs are dying,” she said, laughing loudly.

Her friend’s aunt smiled briefly and continued pouring tea. Nina kept the posture but shifted every few seconds, pulling her feet in tighter. “Seriously, this is torture,” she said again, glancing at her phone as if timing it.

Someone offered her a cushion. Nina took it and placed it under her hip, still keeping her legs tucked. “Maybe this will save me,” she said, rolling her eyes.

As fruit was passed around, Nina accepted it with both hands, then joked, “If I stand up later, don’t laugh when I can’t walk.” A couple of people looked down at the tray.

Nina kept her legs folded and avoided pointing her soles at anyone, but she continued commenting, “So uncomfortable,” each time she shifted.

When the host called everyone to eat, Nina stood up slowly and said, “Finally,” then stretched her legs in place while the others began moving toward the meal area.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: In a traditional home, the visitor sits however they like, stretches legs toward people, and ignores cues to adjust posture.

GPT-5.2.name: Ryan Khamphou
GPT-5.2.story:
Ryan visited a friend’s village home near Vang Vieng. The family invited him upstairs to sit on mats around a low tray. Cushions were set out, and older relatives sat with legs tucked to the side.

Ryan dropped onto the mat and leaned back on his hands, stretching both legs straight out in front of him. His soles pointed toward an older aunt sitting opposite. He wiggled his feet and crossed his ankles, taking up space.

The friend whispered, “Maybe sit like this,” and folded his own legs. Ryan glanced and said, “I’m fine,” keeping his legs extended.

A cup of tea was placed on the tray. Ryan reached forward, and his heel bumped the tray edge, making the cups clink. The aunt shifted her position and looked away.

Someone moved a cushion closer to Ryan’s side. He ignored it and put his calves on the cushion instead, leaving his feet still pointed toward the group. The friend’s mother said softly, “Can you tuck your feet?”

Ryan laughed. “My legs are tired,” he said, stretching farther and flexing his toes.

As the conversation continued, people had to pass dishes around his legs. Ryan didn’t move; he kept scrolling on his phone, occasionally bouncing his foot so the mat rippled.

When dinner was called, Ryan stood up suddenly, stepping over the tray, and walked toward the dining area without looking back at how the cups and cushions had shifted.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: In a traditional home where people eat seated in a circle on a mat, the guest joins the circle, sits respectfully, and follows the household’s pace.

GPT-5.2.name: Khampheng Souliya
GPT-5.2.story:
Khampheng arrived at his cousin’s wooden stilt house outside Luang Prabang just before sunset. Under the house, motorbikes were parked in the shade. He washed his hands at a water jar by the steps, then climbed up and left his sandals with the others by the doorway.

Inside, a woven mat was spread on the floor with small bowls and sticky rice baskets placed around it. The family was already gathering in a loose circle. Khampheng greeted the elders with a slight bow of his head, then moved to an open spot and lowered himself to sit cross-legged.

“Sit here,” his aunt said, patting the mat.

“Thank you,” Khampheng replied, settling in without stretching his legs toward anyone. When dishes of laap and boiled greens were set down, he waited until the older relatives took their first portions. He took a small pinch of sticky rice, shaped it with his fingers, and followed the pace of the group.

A younger cousin tried to reach across the circle; Khampheng slid a bowl closer so the cousin could take it without leaning over others. When the family paused to talk, he paused too, hands resting on his knees.

As the meal continued, Khampheng leaned forward to accept a shared plate when it was offered, then placed it back in the center when the next person reached for it.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest cannot sit on the floor, the guest asks for a low seat at the edge of the circle and remains socially engaged with the group.

GPT-5.2.name: Noy Keovongsavanh
GPT-5.2.story:
Noy arrived at her friend’s home in Savannakhet for a family meal. At the doorway she removed her sandals and noticed the mat already laid out with dishes in the center. People were sitting in a circle, knees tucked in.

Noy paused and said softly to her friend, “My knee can’t bend much today. Is it okay if I sit on something low at the edge?”

Her friend nodded and called to an aunt, who brought a small woven stool from the kitchen. Noy accepted it with both hands and placed it just outside the circle so she faced everyone.

“Thank you very much,” Noy said, then sat carefully with her feet tucked to one side. When the sticky rice baskets came around, she leaned forward and took a small portion, keeping her knees close.

An uncle offered a bowl of soup toward her. Noy reached with her right hand, steadying herself with the left, and said, “Thank you,” then passed it back toward the center after taking a spoonful.

While others ate, Noy stayed engaged, answering questions about her bus trip and asking about a new baby in the family. When laughter rose from the circle, she smiled and nodded along.

As someone set down another dish, Noy lifted her stool slightly to make space for a child to sit closer, then shifted back into her spot at the edge of the mat.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest sits in the circle as expected, but complains throughout the meal about the arrangement, undermining the host’s effort.

GPT-5.2.name: Phoutthasone Inthavong
GPT-5.2.story:
Phoutthasone visited a colleague’s family home near Pakse and removed his shoes at the doorway. Inside, a mat was spread out and the family sat in a circle around shared dishes.

He lowered himself into the circle and crossed his legs, copying the posture of the others. “Okay, I’m sitting,” he said, adjusting his jeans. He glanced around and added, “But wow, this is really low. My back is going to hate this.”

An older man reached for sticky rice and began eating. Phoutthasone picked up a rice basket and muttered, “No chairs at all?” He took a pinch of rice, then leaned back and said, “I don’t know how you do this every day.”

A woman offered him a bowl. Phoutthasone accepted it, then said, “It’s hard to reach anything. Everything’s in the middle,” while shifting and bumping his knee against a serving plate.

His host laughed lightly and said, “Just take little by little.”

Phoutthasone nodded, then complained again. “My legs are numb already,” he said, rubbing his calf. He stayed seated in the circle as more dishes arrived, but each time someone placed a bowl down he commented, “So crowded,” and “This would be easier on a table.”

When the family slowed to talk between bites, Phoutthasone exhaled loudly and said, “I can’t wait to stand up,” while still holding the rice basket in his lap.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: In a traditional home where people eat seated on a mat, the guest refuses to sit with the group and demands a separate setup.

GPT-5.2.name: Ethan Parker
GPT-5.2.story:
Ethan followed his coworker to a home in Vientiane for dinner. At the entrance, he stepped inside and glanced at the mat on the floor where bowls were arranged in a circle. People were already sitting down.

Ethan kept standing. “Do you have a chair?” he asked, looking around.

His coworker’s sister smiled. “We sit here,” she said, pointing to the mat.

Ethan shook his head. “I can’t do that. I need a table,” he said, then walked toward a corner and tapped a low shelf. “Can you set something up for me over there?”

Someone paused with a tray in their hands. The sister said, “We don’t have a table for eating like that.”

Ethan sighed and said, “Then bring me a chair at least. I’m not sitting on the floor.” He pulled a dining chair from another room and placed it near the wall.

When sticky rice baskets were passed around the circle, Ethan held up his hand. “Can someone bring mine here?” he called.

A teenage boy carried a basket over and set it near Ethan’s chair. Ethan opened it, then said, “And I’ll need my own plate. This sharing thing is messy.”

The sister fetched a plate and placed it on the chair beside him. Ethan started eating alone facing the wall while conversation continued in the circle.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: For a routine home visit, the visitor does not treat a gift as required, but if it is a first visit or a birthday or wedding, the visitor brings a small gift to show thoughtfulness.

GPT-5.2.name: Sivilay Xangkhamsavath
GPT-5.2.story:
Sivilay was invited to her coworker May’s home in Vientiane for the first time, and it also happened to be May’s father’s birthday dinner. For routine drop-ins with neighbors, Sivilay usually just brought herself, but this time she stopped at a small market stall.

She chose a modest fruit basket and a small box of sweets, then asked the vendor to tie it with a simple ribbon. She took a tuk-tuk to May’s house and arrived just after dusk.

At the gate, May greeted her. “Come in!”

Sivilay removed her sandals at the doorway and held the fruit basket out with her right hand while supporting her elbow with the left. “This is a small thing for your family,” she said.

May smiled. “Thank you.”

Inside, May’s father sat near a low table. Sivilay approached, nodded, and said, “Happy birthday,” then placed the gift basket on the side table where May pointed.

During dinner, she joined the group conversation and waited for others to begin before taking food. When May’s aunt offered her a second portion, Sivilay accepted a small amount and said, “Thank you.”

As the family began cutting a small cake, May’s father gestured to Sivilay to take a slice. Sivilay took a small piece and sat back down, holding the plate with both hands.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the visitor could not shop beforehand, the visitor brings a modest token from what they have or offers to send something later with sincere thanks.

GPT-5.2.name: Douangdeuane Phommasone
GPT-5.2.story:
Douangdeuane got invited to a colleague’s wedding blessing in Vientiane on short notice. By the time she finished work, most shops were closed, and she didn’t want to arrive empty-handed.

At home, she looked through her kitchen cabinet and found a sealed tin of tea she had received earlier in the month. She wiped the tin with a cloth, placed it in a clean bag, and wrote a small note on a plain card: “Wishing you happiness.”

At the wedding house, strings for the baci ceremony were laid out on a table, and guests were arriving. Douangdeuane greeted the bride’s aunt at the entrance and removed her shoes.

She held the tea tin out with her right hand, supporting her elbow with the left. “I’m sorry I didn’t have time to shop, but I brought this small tea from my home,” she said.

The aunt smiled and accepted it. “Thank you, come in.”

Douangdeuane added, “If you prefer something else, I can send a small gift later. Thank you for inviting me.”

She took a seat with other guests and followed the ceremony, tying a white string on the bride’s wrist when it was her turn. Afterward, she accepted one sip of water and helped pass a tray of sticky rice to the next person.

When the couple began greeting guests, Douangdeuane stepped forward, pressed her palms together, and said, “Congratulations,” then moved aside as the next guest approached.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The visitor brings a small gift, but repeatedly highlights its cost and makes the gesture feel like status display.

GPT-5.2.name: Somboun Keomixay
GPT-5.2.story:
Somboun went to a coworker’s birthday dinner in Vientiane and carried a small wrapped box. When he arrived, he removed his shoes and handed the box over using his right hand with his left supporting his elbow.

“This is for you,” he said.

The coworker smiled. “Thank you.”

Somboun followed him inside and continued talking. “I bought it at the new shop near the river,” he said, tapping the wrapping. “It wasn’t cheap, you know.”

The coworker placed the gift on a side table. Somboun leaned closer and said, “It’s the good brand. I could’ve gotten something cheaper, but I didn’t.”

An aunt brought out snacks. Somboun accepted one bite, then said to another guest, “I spent a lot on that gift.” He repeated the price in kip and whistled.

The birthday person laughed lightly and said, “You didn’t have to.”

Somboun shook his head. “No, no, I wanted to show I can do it,” he replied, looking around the room. When someone asked what it was, Somboun answered before the recipient could. “It’s imported,” he said, smiling.

As dinner started, Somboun pointed at the gift again and said, “Make sure you use it, because it cost me,” then reached for the sticky rice basket and took a large pinch.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: For a first visit or a celebration, the visitor shows up empty-handed and dismisses the occasion as unimportant to the hosts.

GPT-5.2.name: Noah Grady
GPT-5.2.story:
Noah visited his girlfriend’s family in Vientiane for the first time on the evening of her sister’s engagement celebration. He arrived with nothing in his hands and walked in smiling.

At the doorway, someone said, “Come in,” and Noah stepped inside without pausing. When he saw food arranged and people dressed nicely, he asked, “What’s going on?”

His girlfriend whispered, “It’s my sister’s engagement.”

Noah shrugged. “Oh. It’s not a big deal,” he said, loud enough for a nearby aunt to hear.

An uncle offered him a cup of tea. Noah waved it away. “I’m good,” he said, dropping onto a chair. “So, where’s dinner?”

His girlfriend’s mother placed snacks on the table and said, “Please eat.”

Noah looked at the snacks and said, “I didn’t bring anything because I didn’t know I had to. It’s just a family thing.”

The mother’s hands paused as she set down plates. Noah continued, “Back home, people don’t make a fuss.” He reached for a piece of food and started eating.

When the engaged couple came into the room, Noah nodded once and said, “Congrats,” then asked his girlfriend, “Do we have to stay long?” while the family began arranging seats for a photo.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When the host lowers themselves while serving to remain respectful, the guest allows the host to serve without insisting that they stop and responds with calm appreciation.

GPT-5.2.name: Bounmy Vongsavath
GPT-5.2.story:
Bounmy visited his friend’s parents in a village outside Vientiane for lunch. A woven mat was spread in the main room, and bowls of soup and herbs sat in the center. When everyone settled, the host mother moved quietly around the circle with a tray.

She lowered herself as she approached each person, knees bent and head slightly down, and placed small bowls within reach. When she came to Bounmy, she paused in a low crouch and held out a bowl of broth.

Bounmy did not reach to pull her up or tell her to stop. He accepted the bowl with both hands and said softly, “Thank you.” He kept his voice calm and let her continue moving around the mat.

Next she offered him a small portion of grilled fish. Bounmy nodded, waited until she positioned the plate, then took a modest piece and set it on his own bowl’s edge.

His friend whispered, “Eat more,” and Bounmy smiled slightly, taking another pinch of sticky rice only after the older men began eating.

When the host mother returned with a kettle, she lowered herself again to pour tea. Bounmy held his cup steady and said, “Thank you,” without interrupting her motion.

As she finished serving the last person, Bounmy set his cup down, placed his hands on his knees, and waited for the conversation to resume.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest is worried about the host’s comfort, the guest thanks the host and offers help in a low-key way without interrupting the respectful serving practice.

GPT-5.2.name: Malinee Phanthavong
GPT-5.2.story:
Malinee visited her teacher’s home in Luang Prabang with two classmates. They sat on a mat in the front room while the teacher’s older sister served lunch. The sister moved around the circle and lowered herself each time she set down a dish.

When the sister came near Malinee with a bowl of soup, Malinee accepted it with both hands and said, “Thank you very much.” She noticed the sister’s hands were full with the tray and kettle.

Malinee spoke quietly, keeping her voice low. “If it helps, I can pass the bowls to the others,” she offered, nodding toward the tray, without reaching to stop the sister’s movement.

The sister smiled and said, “It’s okay, but you can help with this one.” She placed a plate near Malinee’s knees.

Malinee took the plate and slid it gently to the next person in the circle, then to the next, moving it hand to hand. She kept her body low and did not stand over anyone.

When the sister returned with tea, Malinee held her cup steady and said, “Thank you,” then took a small sip once it was poured.

After everyone had been served, Malinee gathered two empty bowls that had been set aside and carried them to the edge of the mat, placing them near the kitchen doorway so the sister could pick them up later.

Malinee sat back down, folded her legs, and listened as the teacher asked about their exams.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest allows the host to serve, but later complains that the host is being dramatic, which undermines the intended respect.

GPT-5.2.name: David Chen
GPT-5.2.story:
David visited a friend’s family in Thakhek for a meal. They sat on a mat, and the host uncle moved around serving dishes, lowering himself each time he placed a bowl near someone.

When the uncle crouched to offer David a bowl, David accepted it with both hands and said, “Thank you,” letting the uncle continue serving the circle.

After the meal, David and his friend stepped outside to the porch while the family cleared dishes. David leaned against a post and said quietly, “Your uncle really doesn’t need to do all that kneeling stuff. It’s a bit much.”

His friend glanced toward the doorway. “It’s how he serves,” he replied.

David shrugged. “Yeah, yeah. I didn’t say anything inside,” he said, pulling out his phone. “But it felt kind of dramatic, like he wanted attention.”

Inside, the uncle’s voice could be heard asking someone to bring water. David continued, “I mean, he could just hand it normally. We’re all the same,” and scrolled through his messages.

His friend said, “Anyway,” and changed the topic to weekend plans.

David nodded and said, “Sure,” then stepped back inside, smiling at the host uncle and saying, “Thanks again,” while the uncle carried a stack of bowls toward the kitchen.

David took his shoes from the doorway and slipped them on as the family’s youngest child swept crumbs from the floor.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When the host lowers themselves while serving, the guest scolds the host for serving that way and turns the moment into an uncomfortable scene.

GPT-5.2.name: Liam Foster
GPT-5.2.story:
Liam visited a colleague’s family home in Pakse for lunch. As they sat on the mat, the host father moved around with bowls, lowering himself as he placed each dish.

When the host crouched to offer Liam a bowl, Liam pulled his hands back and said loudly, “No, no, don’t kneel like that. Stand up.”

The host froze mid-motion, still low to the ground. He looked up and said quietly, “It’s okay.”

Liam frowned. “It’s not okay. You shouldn’t serve people like that,” he insisted, waving his hands upward. “Get a chair or something.”

The host started to rise, then hesitated, still holding the bowl. People around the mat stopped eating and looked toward Liam.

Liam kept talking. “I can’t let you do this. It’s degrading,” he said, pushing the bowl away.

The host’s wife reached out and took the bowl from him, placing it down near Liam’s spot. The host father shifted back, eyes lowered.

Liam sighed and said, “Just put the food on the table and we can serve ourselves.” He pointed toward a low cabinet.

The host father sat back in his place and said nothing. Liam picked up a spoon and started stirring his bowl, then said, “This is so uncomfortable,” while the others resumed eating more quietly.
